name: Ace of Cups
meaning_upright: The Ace of Cups is the heart overflowing: new love, deep compassion, or an emotional and spiritual awakening. It invites receiving and giving feeling freely, without rationing.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Ace shows the cup tipped or blocked — emotions withheld, love unexpressed, or an inner emptiness asking for attention. Refilling starts with self-compassion.
love: In love, it is one of the strongest cards for new romance or a fresh emotional depth in an existing bond.
career: In career, it signals emotionally fulfilling work, creative flow, or warm new connections.
mood: The mood is tender and open-hearted — feelings close to the surface, in the best way.
spiritual: Spiritually, the Ace of Cups is love as opening: the heart offered and refilled from a source deeper than circumstance.

name: Two of Cups
meaning_upright: The Two of Cups is mutual recognition: two people meeting as equals in attraction, friendship, or alliance. It marks a genuine bond where feeling flows both ways.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows a connection out of balance — one cup fuller than the other, trust strained, or harmony disrupted. Repair begins with honest, equal exchange.
love: In love, it is the card of mutual attraction and partnership — a bond of genuine reciprocity.
career: In career, it favors partnerships, collaborations, and one-on-one alliances built on mutual respect.
mood: The mood is affectionate and harmonious — met, mirrored, understood.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is sacred connection: another person as a mirror in which the soul recognizes itself.

name: Three of Cups
meaning_upright: The Three of Cups is friendship and shared joy — the toast among people who genuinely celebrate each other. It encourages leaning into community and marking wins together.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it warns of overindulgence, gossip, or a third presence straining a bond. It can also signal withdrawal from friends when their support is most needed.
love: In love, it brings celebration and social warmth, though reversed it can flag outside interference in the relationship.
career: In career, it signals successful teamwork and collaborative wins worth celebrating.
mood: The mood is joyful and sociable — laughter shared, cups raised.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is communion: gratitude multiplied by being celebrated among kindred spirits.

name: Four of Cups
meaning_upright: The Four of Cups is emotional weariness: arms crossed, absorbed in discontent, while a new cup is offered unseen. It asks whether the boredom is real or whether attention has simply turned away from what's available.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it marks the end of apathy — noticing the offered cup at last and re-engaging with life. Motivation returns as attention comes back to the present.
love: In love, it suggests emotional distance or taking a partner for granted; an offer of connection may be going unnoticed.
career: In career, it signals disengagement or an overlooked opportunity — look again before saying nothing interests you.
mood: The mood is apathetic and withdrawn — full cups nearby, appetite gone.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is a call to presence: noticing the blessing being offered before deciding nothing is there.

name: Five of Cups
meaning_upright: The Five of Cups is grief fixed on the three spilled cups while two still stand full behind. The loss is real and deserves mourning — and the card gently notes that not everything is lost.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it marks the turn toward healing: accepting what happened, forgiving, and finally seeing the cups still standing. Recovery has begun.
love: In love, it points to grieving a loss or disappointment — and to the remaining connection that grief may be obscuring.
career: In career, it signals a setback worth mourning briefly, then salvaging — something valuable survived the spill.
mood: The mood is sorrowful and regretful — attention fixed on what spilled.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is grief as teacher: mourning honestly, then turning to the cups that still stand.

name: Six of Cups
meaning_upright: The Six of Cups is sweet memory and simple kindness — nostalgia, reunions, and the healing comfort of the familiar. It can herald someone from the past returning or old joys revisited.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it warns of living in a rose-tinted past at the present's expense. It can also mark the healthy release of old ties and childhood patterns.
love: In love, it evokes innocent affection, a past lover resurfacing, or reconnecting with why you fell for each other.
career: In career, it can signal returning to earlier passions or working with something from your past.
mood: The mood is nostalgic and sweet — memory softening the present.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is inner-child healing: revisiting innocence to reclaim the simple kindness the soul remembers.

name: Seven of Cups
meaning_upright: The Seven of Cups is a cloud of glittering options, some real and some mirage. It warns against choosing by fantasy alone — examine what each cup actually contains before reaching.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the fog lifts: illusions dissolve, priorities sharpen, and a real choice gets made. Commitment replaces daydreaming.
love: In love, it cautions against idealizing someone or keeping options endlessly open instead of choosing.
career: In career, it signals many possibilities but a need for realistic evaluation — pick one and commit.
mood: The mood is dreamy and indecisive — many doors, all glittering.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is discernment: asking which vision feeds the soul and which merely fascinates it.

name: Eight of Cups
meaning_upright: The Eight of Cups is the quiet departure: leaving behind something built because it no longer feeds the soul. It honors the courage to seek deeper meaning even at real cost.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows the departure stalled — knowing it's time to go but staying, or drifting away without truly deciding. It can also mark a return to give things one more chance.
love: In love, it signals emotionally outgrowing a situation and the difficult choice to walk away or recommit fully.
career: In career, it points to leaving a stable but unfulfilling path in search of meaningful work.
mood: The mood is wistful and restless — the quiet ache of outgrowing something.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is the soul's quest: leaving the comfortable shore because meaning has moved elsewhere.

name: Nine of Cups
meaning_upright: The Nine of Cups is the wish card: satisfaction, comfort, and desires coming to fruition. It invites genuine enjoyment and gratitude for what has been achieved.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it questions the satisfaction on display — pleasure that doesn't nourish, smugness, or wishes granted that weren't the right wishes. Fulfillment needs realigning with what actually matters.
love: In love, it signals emotional contentment and wishes in matters of the heart coming true.
career: In career, it marks well-earned satisfaction and rewards — enjoy the achievement without coasting.
mood: The mood is content and satisfied — wishes landing, comfort earned.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is gratitude's test: enjoying fulfillment while asking what truly nourishes beneath the pleasure.

name: Ten of Cups
meaning_upright: The Ten of Cups is the rainbow over the family: lasting emotional fulfillment, harmony at home, and love realized in daily life. It represents the happy ending that is also a way of living.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, it shows the picture and the reality diverging — tension behind the family portrait or ideals straining real relationships. Reconnection asks for honesty over performance.
love: In love, it is a card of deep commitment, family, and shared happiness — the full emotional arc.
career: In career, it signals work-life harmony and an environment that feels like belonging.
mood: The mood is loving and fulfilled — harmony that feels like home.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is love realized: everyday life itself becoming the blessing that was once only wished for.

name: Page of Cups
meaning_upright: The Page of Cups is the tender dreamer bearing an unexpected gift — a sweet message, a creative impulse, or a first stirring of feeling. It encourages openness to whimsy and intuition.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Page turns moody or escapist: feelings expressed sideways, creativity blocked, or sensitivity tipping into sulking. The emotions are valid; the expression needs maturing.
love: In love, it brings sweet gestures, budding feelings, or a sensitive admirer.
career: In career, it signals creative beginnings and intuitive nudges worth taking seriously.
mood: The mood is playful and sensitive — imagination bubbling up unannounced.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is budding intuition: whimsical messages from the heart that deserve to be taken seriously.

name: Knight of Cups
meaning_upright: The Knight of Cups rides in with an offer of the heart — romance, an invitation, or an ideal pursued with genuine feeling. It favors following the heart while keeping one foot on the ground.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Knight's charm outpaces his substance: beautiful words without follow-through, or idealism curdling into disappointment. Judge by actions, not poetry.
love: In love, he is the classic romantic — courtship, proposals, and grand feeling — though reversed his promises need testing.
career: In career, he brings creative proposals and appealing offers; verify the practical details.
mood: The mood is romantic and idealistic — led by the heart, gladly.
spiritual: Spiritually, it is the heart's calling: following feeling as a compass while keeping one foot on the earth.

name: Queen of Cups
meaning_upright: The Queen of Cups is deep water held in a graceful vessel: profound empathy, intuition, and emotional wisdom. She listens beneath words and nurtures without drowning.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the Queen loses her shoreline — absorbing everyone's feelings, giving past empty, or using emotion to steer others. Boundaries restore her gift.
love: In love, she embodies emotional attunement and devoted care — a bond where feelings are truly heard.
career: In career, she favors empathic leadership, counseling, and work guided by emotional intelligence.
mood: The mood is empathic and calm — deep waters, gently held.
spiritual: Spiritually, she is compassion embodied: emotional depth as a gift that heals others without drowning the self.

name: King of Cups
meaning_upright: The King of Cups sails turbulent seas without capsizing: emotional mastery, diplomacy, and compassion held in steady balance. He advises leading with calm heart rather than reaction.
meaning_reversed: Reversed, the King's calm is a lid: repressed feelings leaking as coldness, moodiness, or quiet manipulation. Mastery means feeling fully, not feeling less.
love: In love, he is the emotionally steady partner — supportive and composed, provided the depths are shared, not sealed.
career: In career, he signals wise counsel and diplomacy — staying gracious and level under pressure.
mood: The mood is steady and gracious — feelings present, never in charge.
spiritual: Spiritually, he is mastery of the heart: wisdom that comes from feeling everything and being ruled by none of it.
